No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet.

To find the speed of the sailboat when instantaneous wind power is maximized, we must first express power mathematically. Power \( N \) equals force \( F \) multiplied by the boat's speed \( v \). Substituting the given force formula into the power equation yields \( N = \frac{B S \rho (v_0 - v)^2 v}{2} \). Since \( B \), \( S \), \( \rho \), and \( 2 \) are constants, maximizing power is equivalent to maximizing the function \( f(v) = (v_0 - v)^2 v \). We expand this expression to \( f(v) = (v_0^2 - 2v_0v + v^2)v = v_0^2v - 2v_0v^2 + v^3 \). To locate the maximum, we take the derivative of \( f(v) \) with respect to \( v \) and set it to zero. The derivative is \( f'(v) = v_0^2 - 4v_0v + 3v^2 \). Setting this equal to zero gives the quadratic equation \( 3v^2 - 4v_0v + v_0^2 = 0 \). Factoring this equation results in \( (3v - v_0)(v - v_0) = 0 \). This provides two possible solutions: \( v = v_0 \) or \( v = \frac{v_0}{3} \). If \( v = v_0 \), the force becomes zero, resulting in zero power, which is a minimum. Therefore, the maximum power occurs at \( v = \frac{v_0}{3} \). Substituting the given value \( v_0 = 6.3 \, \text{m/s} \) into this result yields \( v = \frac{6.3}{3} \). Calculating this division gives \( 2.1 \). The speed of the sailboat at the moment of maximum instantaneous wind power is \( 2.1 \, \text{m/s} \).
